definition: A primitive root; to have affection for (sexually or otherwise): — (be-)love(-d, -ly, -r), like, friend.

semantic_categories.name: Covenantal love toward God
semantic_categories.description: Commanded love for YHWH, the core relational obligation of the covenant. Paired with fearing, serving, and keeping commandments. The Shema defines this as total-person devotion (heart, soul, might).

semantic_categories.name: God's covenantal love toward people
semantic_categories.description: God's elective, initiating affection — choosing Israel, keeping covenant, and acting in mercy. Often grounded in prior commitment (patriarchs) rather than Israel's merit.

semantic_categories.name: Interpersonal love
semantic_categories.description: Human-to-human affection including parental, spousal, and friendship bonds. Covers the full spectrum from Isaac/Rebekah to Jonathan/David to the neighbor commandment.

semantic_categories.name: Romantic and erotic love
semantic_categories.description: Passionate desire between lovers. Most concentrated in Song of Solomon, but present in narratives (Jacob/Rachel, Samson/Delilah, Amnon/Tamar).

semantic_categories.name: Love for wisdom, righteousness, and Torah
semantic_categories.description: Affection directed toward abstract goods — wisdom personified, God's law, justice, and truth. Concentrated in Psalms and Proverbs.

semantic_categories.name: God's love for righteousness and justice
semantic_categories.description: YHWH as subject loving specific moral qualities — righteousness, justice, the righteous — and hating their opposites.

semantic_categories.name: Misdirected love (idolatry and unfaithfulness)
semantic_categories.description: Israel pursuing 'lovers' (foreign gods, alliances) instead of YHWH. The prophetic metaphor of spiritual adultery. The participle form 'lovers' (meahavim) becomes almost a technical term for false objects of devotion.

semantic_categories.name: Love of wrong things (moral warning)
semantic_categories.description: Loving death, sleep, pleasure, violence, transgression — Proverbs' wisdom literature warnings against disordered affection.

collocations.pattern: love the LORD / the LORD loves

synonyms.distinction: delight in, take pleasure in — volitional pleasure vs. ahab's broader affection
to cherish, embrace — rare (1x), tender affection
to accept, be pleased with — divine acceptance/favor rather than emotional love

antonyms.distinction: to hate — the primary opposite; forms a standard contrast pair (loved/hated)
to abhor, detest — stronger rejection than sane; moral revulsion

word_family.relationship: feminine noun form — love (abstract concept, used in Song of Solomon and covenant contexts)
